children.heading: Historical park
children.label: 1 Historical park
children.text: The term “historical park” means the Harriet Tubman National Historical Park established by subsection (b)(1)(A).

children.heading: Home
children.label: 2 Home
children.text: The term “Home” means The Harriet Tubman Home, Inc., located in Auburn, New York.

children.heading: Map
children.label: 3 Map
children.text: The term “map” means the map entitled “Harriet Tubman National Historical Park”, numbered T18/80,000, and dated March 2009.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 4 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: State
children.label: 5 State
children.text: The term “State” means the State of New York.

children.heading: Harriet Tubman National Historical Park
children.label: b Harriet Tubman National Historical Park

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), there is established the Harriet Tubman National Historical Park in Auburn, New York, as a unit of the National Park System.

children.heading: Determination by Secretary
children.label: B Determination by Secretary
children.text: The historical park shall not be established until the date on which the Secretary determines that a sufficient quantity of land, or interests in land, has been acquired to constitute a manageable park unit.

children.heading: Notice
children.label: C Notice
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date on which the Secretary makes a determination under subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register notice of the establishment of the historical park.

children.heading: Map
children.label: D Map
children.text: The map shall be on file and available for public inspection in appropriate offices of the National Park Service.

children.heading: Boundary
children.label: 2 Boundary
children.text: The historical park shall include the Harriet Tubman Home, the Tubman Home for the Aged, the Thompson Memorial AME Zion Church and Rectory, and associated land, as identified in the area entitled “National Historical Park Proposed Boundary” on the map.

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 3 Purpose
children.text: The purpose of the historical park is to preserve and interpret for the benefit of present and future generations the historical, cultural, and natural resources associated with the life of Harriet Tubman.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may acquire land and interests in land within the areas depicted on the map by purchase from a willing seller, donation, or exchange.

children.heading: No condemnation
children.label: B No condemnation
children.text: No land or interest in land within the areas depicted on the map may be acquired by condemnation.

children.label: A
children.text: the National Park System Organic Act ( et seq.); and16 U.S.C. 11See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: the Act of ( et seq.).August 21, 193516 U.S.C. 4611

children.heading: Interpretive tours
children.label: 2 Interpretive tours
children.text: The Secretary may provide interpretive tours to sites and resources located outside the boundary of the historical park in Auburn, New York, relating to the life of Harriet Tubman.

children.label: I
children.text: access at reasonable times by historical park visitors to the land; and

children.label: II
children.text: interpretation of the land for the public; and

children.label: ii
children.text: no changes or alterations shall be made to the land except by mutual agreement of the Secretary and the owner of the land.

children.heading: Research
children.label: B Research
children.text: The Secretary may enter into an agreement with the State, political subdivisions of the State, institutions of higher education, the Home and other nonprofit organizations, and individuals to conduct research relating to the life of Harriet Tubman.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: i Federal share
children.text: The Federal share of the total cost of any activity carried out under this paragraph shall not exceed 50 percent.

children.heading: Form of non-Federal share
children.label: ii Form of non-Federal share
children.text: The non-Federal share may be in the form of in-kind contributions or goods or services fairly valued.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall submit to the Attorney General for review any agreement under this paragraph involving religious property or property owned by a religious institution.

children.heading: Finding
children.label: ii Finding
children.text: No agreement subject to review under this subparagraph shall take effect until the date on which the Attorney General issues a finding that the proposed agreement does not violate the Establishment Clause of the first amendment to the Constitution.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 3 years after the date on which funds are made available to carry out this section, the Secretary shall prepare a general management plan for the historical park in accordance with .section 100502 of title 54

children.label: A
children.text: the Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad National Historical Park established by ; andsection 410sss of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the National Underground Railroad Network to Freedom.
